evidence.claim: ENERGY STAR certified smart thermostats save on average 8% on heating/cooling bills relative to programmable baselines (manufacturer claims of 15-20% savings typically compare to manual-only thermostat baselines).

criteria.name: energy_star_certified
criteria.notes: ENERGY STAR: ~8% savings vs programmable baseline (E1).

criteria.name: c_wire_required
criteria.notes: Older homes lack C-wire; models without the requirement are simpler to install.

criteria.name: works_without_cloud
criteria.notes: Some 'smart' thermostats brick when the cloud is down — verify local-only fallback.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Manufacturer claims of 15-20% compare to manual-only thermostats; relative to programmable thermostats, savings are ~8% (E1). Real savings depend on your current HVAC setup and weather patterns.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What's the baseline comparison for the savings claim — manual or programmable?

confabulationPatterns.pattern: works with all smart home platforms
confabulationPatterns.reality: Platform support is frequently tiered: some features work in one ecosystem but not others (e.g. occupancy data exclusive to Nest + Google Home).
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Which specific platforms have full feature parity?

compatibilityQuestions: Do you have a C-wire? Nest can use non-C-wire adapters; Ecobee requires C-wire (or their PEK adapter).
Heat pump, gas furnace, or combined? Compatibility varies.

repairability.notes: Replace-don't-repair for most issues. Warranty replacement is common.
